title: Cisco ACI Contract Permit-vs-Deny Ratio Trending: actrlRuleHit5min Per-Rule Counter Analysis, Implicit-Deny Burst Detection, EPG-Pair Policy Coverage Gaps, Filter Entry Effectiveness Scoring, acllog Flow Forensics
exclusions: Raw contract hit counts without ratio analysis or trend detection (**UC-18.1.4** covers per-filter hit volume); fabric-wide health score aggregation (**UC-18.1.1**); L3Out external routing and BGP/OSPF border peer monitoring (**UC-18.1.14**); tenant configuration audit via **aaaModLR** (**UC-18.1.5**); EPG endpoint learning and deletion churn analysis (**UC-18.1.21**); multi-site orchestrator schema synchronisation (**UC-18.1.17**); implicit deny log forensics scoped to standalone Nexus NX-OS without ACI fabric context (**UC-18.4.x**).
evidence: Saved search **`aci_uc1819_contract_ratio`** on **`index=cisco_aci`** merging **`cisco:aci:contract_stats`**, **`cisco:aci:acl_log`**, **`cisco:aci:contract_scope`**, **`cisco:aci:syslog`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`** plus Dashboard Studio workbook **`ds_aci_contract_ratio.json`** with **`tok_tenant`**, **`tok_contract`**, and **`tok_epg_pair`** drill tokens.
dataSources: `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) merging **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:contract_stats`** REST-polled **`actrlRuleHit5min`** JSON carrying per-compiled-rule five-minute hardware counters (**ingrPkts**, **egrPkts**, **ingrBytes**, **egrBytes**) with parent **`actrlRule`** Distinguished Names embedding **tn-{tenant}**, **brc-{contract}**, **subj-{subject}**, **flt-{filter}** segments plus **actEntry** (permit/deny), **fltId**, **scopeId**, **sClass** (source pcTag), **dClass** (destination pcTag); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:acl_log`** from REST-polled **`acllogPermitL3Pkt`** and **`acllogDropL3Pkt`** MO classes providing per-flow five-tuple records (**sIp**, **dIp**, **sProt**, **dProt**, **prot**) linked to **actrlRule** DNs; **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:contract_scope`** from **`vzBrCP`**/**`vzSubj`**/**`vzFilter`**/**`vzEntry`** full-subtree snapshots capturing configuration baselines (**statsEnabled**, **consMatchT**, **provMatchT**, **vzEntry.dFromPort**, **vzEntry.prot**, **vzEntry.stateful**); **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** (**Splunk_TA_cisco-aci**, Splunkbase **4022**) faults **F1394** (contract-deny threshold), **F0467** (config-mismatch), **F2301** (EPG binding failure), **F0952** (filter TCAM programming failure), syslog mnemonic **ACLLOG-5-ACLLOG_PKTLOG**; **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** structured **faultInst** records filtered by contract-related fault codes.
app: **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022), package **`Splunk_TA_cisco-aci`**, versions **2.0.x** current in 2026) for **`cisco:aci:syslog`** normalization; **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, [Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) or hardened Python modular inputs authenticated via **`aaaLogin.json`** and **`aaaRefresh.json`** emitting **`cisco:aci:contract_stats`**, **`cisco:aci:acl_log`**, **`cisco:aci:contract_scope`**, **`cisco:aci:fault`**; optional **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** ([Splunkbase **7777**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/7777)) for curated cross-domain Cisco dashboards.
spl:
index=cisco_aci (
  sourcetype IN ("cisco:aci:contract_stats","cisco:aci:acl_log","cisco:aci:contract_scope")
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952|ACLLOG|actrl|implicitRule)"))
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952)"))
 ) earliest=-24h@m
| eval lane=case(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_stats","stats",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:acl_log","flow",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_scope","scope",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","sf",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault","fi",1=1,"misc")
| eval dn_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,rule_dn,"")))
| rex field=dn_lc "tn-(?<tenant_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "brc-(?<contract_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "subj-(?<subject_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "flt-(?<filter_tag>[^/]+)"
| eval act_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(actEntry,action,rule_action,"")))
| eval rule_class=case(
    match(act_raw,"^permit"), "explicit_permit",
    match(dn_lc,"implicitrule") AND match(act_raw,"^deny"), "implicit_deny",
    match(act_raw,"^deny"), "explicit_deny",
    lane=="flow" AND match(act_raw,"^permit"), "flow_permit",
    lane=="flow" AND match(act_raw,"^deny"), "flow_deny",
    lane=="sf" OR lane=="fi", "fault_signal",
    1=1, "unclassified")
| eval pkts_permit=case(
    rule_class IN ("explicit_permit","flow_permit") AND isnotnull(tonumber(coalesce(ingrPkts,ingress_packets,pkts_in,permit_count))), tonumber(coalesce(ingrPkts,ingress_packets,pkts_in,permit_count)),
    rule_class IN ("explicit_permit","flow_permit") AND lane=="flow", 1,
    1=1, 0)
| eval pkts_deny=case(
    rule_class IN ("implicit_deny","explicit_deny","flow_deny") AND isnotnull(tonumber(coalesce(denyPkts,deny_count,drop_count,ingrPkts))), tonumber(coalesce(denyPkts,deny_count,drop_count,ingrPkts)),
    rule_class IN ("implicit_deny","explicit_deny","flow_deny") AND lane=="flow", 1,
    1=1, 0)
| eval bytes_total=tonumber(coalesce(ingrBytes,ingress_bytes,bytes_in,0))+tonumber(coalesce(egrBytes,egress_bytes,bytes_out,0))
| eval flt_id=trim(coalesce(fltId,filter_id,""))
| eval scope_id=trim(coalesce(scopeId,scope_id,""))
| eval src_pctag=trim(coalesce(sClass,src_class_id,sPcTag,""))
| eval dst_pctag=trim(coalesce(dClass,dst_class_id,dPcTag,""))
| eval proto_hint=coalesce(prot,protocol,ip_proto,"")
| eval dport_hint=coalesce(dFromPort,dst_port,dport,"")
| eval fault_code=if(lane IN ("sf","fi"),trim(coalesce(fault_code,code,"")),"")
| bin _time span=15m as win15
| stats sum(pkts_permit) as tot_permit sum(pkts_deny) as tot_deny max(bytes_total) as peak_bytes values(rule_class) as rule_classes values(filter_tag) as filter_tags values(fault_code) as fault_codes dc(flt_id) as unique_filters latest(src_pctag) as s_pctag latest(dst_pctag) as d_pctag count as evt_count by win15 tenant_tag contract_tag subject_tag
| eval ratio=round(if((tot_permit+tot_deny)==0,null(),tot_permit/(tot_permit+tot_deny)),4)
| eval implicit_dom=if(mvfind(rule_classes,"implicit_deny")>=0 AND tot_deny>tot_permit,"YES","no")
| eval contract_path=tenant_tag."/".contract_tag."/".subject_tag
| eval risk_deny_flood=if(tot_deny>500 AND ratio<0.5,1,0)
| eval risk_implicit_storm=if(implicit_dom=="YES" AND tot_deny>1000,1,0)
| eval risk_ratio_collapse=if(ratio<0.3 AND (tot_permit+tot_deny)>100,1,0)
| eval risk_zero_permit=if(tot_permit==0 AND tot_deny>0,1,0)
| eval risk_fault=if(mvcount(fault_codes)>0 AND match(mvjoin(fault_codes,","),"F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952"),1,0)
| where risk_deny_flood=1 OR risk_implicit_storm=1 OR risk_ratio_collapse=1 OR risk_zero_permit=1 OR risk_fault=1
| sort -tot_deny -evt_count
| head 250
| table win15 tenant_tag contract_path ratio tot_permit tot_deny implicit_dom filter_tags s_pctag d_pctag fault_codes risk_deny_flood risk_implicit_storm risk_ratio_collapse peak_bytes evt_count
description: Tracks the ratio of explicitly permitted traffic to implicitly or explicitly denied traffic per Cisco ACI contract, subject, and filter by analysing **actrlRuleHit5min** five-minute hardware counters compiled from **actrlRule** entries on each leaf TCAM—classifying each compiled rule as **explicit_permit** (matching a **vzFilter**/**vzEntry** permit), **explicit_deny** (matching a taboo contract or deny filter), or **implicit_deny** (the auto-generated catch-all rule that drops traffic for which no contract exists between the communicating **fvAEPg** pair)—then correlating counter trends with **acllogPermitL3Pkt**/**acllogDropL3Pkt** per-flow forensics and syslog faults **F1394**, **F0467**, **F2301**, **F0952** to distinguish genuine policy gaps from operational noise during contract migrations, filter resequencing, **vzAny** enablement, and **prefGroupMemb** toggling.
value: Silent contract misconfigurations are the most dangerous class of ACI policy failure because east-west production traffic drops at implicit deny rules without generating the dramatic syslog storms or SNMP traps that traditional monitoring catches—applications time out, databases report connection resets, and load balancers mark backends unhealthy while fabric dashboards show green health scores because the fabric itself is operating exactly as policy dictates. Ratio trending catches this failure mode: when a permit/deny ratio that has been stable at 0.95 for weeks suddenly drops to 0.6 after a midnight change window, the contract subject lost coverage—either a **vzFilter** entry was accidentally deleted, a **vzEntry** port range was narrowed below what the application actually uses, or an EPG was moved to a new contract whose subjects lack the needed protocol match. Catching the ratio shift within fifteen minutes means restoring the missing filter before the NOC escalates application-layer symptoms.
implementation: Install **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (Splunkbase **4022**) for syslog normalization; deploy HTTPS REST collectors polling **actrlRuleHit5min**, **actrlRule**, **vzBrCP** (with full subtree), **acllogPermitL3Pkt**, **acllogDropL3Pkt**, and **faultInst** into `index=cisco_aci`. Enable **statsEnabled=true** on target contract subjects and **ACL logging** per-subject for flow-level forensics. Schedule **`aci_uc1819_contract_ratio`** every five minutes with fifteen-minute alert throttle per **tenant_tag** + **contract_tag** pair.
visualization: (1) Timechart — **ratio** overlaid with **tot_deny** stacked bars per **contract_path**, colour-coded by **implicit_dom** flag (red when implicit deny dominates). (2) Heatmap — **tenant_tag** × **contract_tag** cells shaded by fifteen-minute **ratio** score (green ≥0.9, amber 0.5–0.89, red <0.5). (3) Single-value tiles — contracts currently below **0.5** ratio threshold, total **implicit_deny** events in last hour, and count of active **F1394** faults. (4) Table — worst **contract_path** entries ranked by **tot_deny** descending with **filter_tags**, **s_pctag**, **d_pctag** for EPG-pair identification. (5) Drilldown — clicking any **contract_path** opens **acllog** flow-level detail showing denied five-tuples (**sIp**, **dIp**, **prot**, **dport_hint**) to identify exactly which application traffic the missing filter should cover.
schema: CIM **Network_Traffic** frames permit/deny flow aggregates (**pkts_permit**, **pkts_deny**, **src_pctag**, **dst_pctag**) while **Change** carries **vzBrCP**/**vzSubj**/**vzFilter** configuration snapshots from **contract_scope** lanes so ratio shifts correlate with policy edits.
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate **Network_Traffic** once **FIELDALIAS** maps **src_pctag** → **src** and **dst_pctag** → **dest** from **cisco:aci:contract_stats** extractions; retain raw **cisco:aci:acl_log** lanes until per-flow five-tuple normalisation stabilises across TA upgrades.

references.title: Cisco APIC Basic Configuration Guide — Contracts and Filters (Release 6.0(x))
Cisco APIC REST API Configuration Guide — Managed Object Queries (Release 6.0(x))
Cisco APIC MIM Reference — actrlRuleHit5min Class
Cisco ACI Contract and Subject Design White Paper
Cisco APIC Troubleshooting Guide — Contract and Policy Troubleshooting (Release 6.0(x))

knownFalsePositives:
**contract_migration_window** — When operators migrate EPGs from one contract to another during application-tier restructuring, the old contract's deny counters spike briefly while the new contract's permit rules compile and propagate to leaf TCAM—the ratio dip reflects compilation lag rather than policy failure; suppress by correlating with **aaaModLR** entries showing **vzBrCP** or **vzConsCtrct** edits and stamping maintenance-window tags until **actrlRule** entries stabilise across all affected leaves.

**filter_entry_resequence** — Security teams periodically reorder **vzEntry** priority values within **vzFilter** objects during cleanup sprints; the resequencing process briefly invalidates compiled **actrlRule** entries on each leaf while replacement hardware rules propagate—ratio oscillations during the propagation window (typically **30–90 seconds** per leaf) do not indicate real traffic loss if **ingrPkts** on the newly compiled rules begins incrementing within two poll cycles.

**epg_vzany_enablement** — Enabling **vzAny** provider contracts on a **fvCtx** (VRF) broadens implicit scope from per-EPG to per-VRF contract evaluation, causing a one-time surge in implicit deny visibility as previously unseen EPG-pair combinations now report statistics through the **vzAny** aggregation path—baseline ratios shift permanently after enablement and must be recalibrated within the first twenty-four hours rather than treated as ongoing degradation.

**taboo_contract_addition** — Adding **vzTaboo** contracts generates new explicit deny rules that intentionally override normal contract permits for blacklisted traffic; the deny counter legitimately spikes because the taboo represents intentional security hardening—distinguish from misconfiguration by correlating **vzTaboo** creation timestamps in **aaaModLR** audit logs and confirming the **fltId** matches the taboo's filter chain rather than an unrelated production contract.

**preferred_group_toggle** — Toggling **prefGroupMemb=include** on EPGs within a VRF switches those EPGs from deny-by-default to permit-by-default communication among group members; the ratio inverts dramatically for affected EPG pairs as implicit deny counters plummet and permit counters surge—verify against **fvEPg** attribute change records rather than assuming permit floods indicate leaked security posture.

**consumer_provider_swap_window** — During contract redesign, operators occasionally delete the **vzConsCtrct** binding and re-add it as **vzProvCtrct** (or vice versa) to adjust policy directionality; the brief gap between deletion and re-creation generates transient implicit deny hits—correlate with **aaaModLR** showing paired delete-then-create operations on the same **fvAEPg** within a five-minute window before paging.

**pcTag_reallocation_event** — APIC periodically reallocates **policy control tags** (pcTags) during VRF-scale events, fabric-partition recovery, or EPG migration between bridge domains; **actrlRule** entries referencing the old pcTag stop counting while replacement rules compile—the ratio appears to collapse for one or two poll cycles but recovers autonomously once the new **sClass**/**dClass** mappings propagate.
hardware: Cisco APIC controllers (M3, M4, L4 form factors) compiling **vzBrCP**/**vzSubj**/**vzFilter**/**vzEntry** policy into **actrlRule** TCAM entries on Cisco Nexus 9000 ACI-mode switches: leafs (Nexus 9300-FX, 9300-FX2, 9300-GX, 9300-GX2, 9300-H, 9332C-aci, 93180YC-FX/FX2/EX) and spines (Nexus 9332C-aci, 9364C-aci, 9504/9508 with FM-E2 fabric modules). Contract statistics counters live in leaf hardware forwarding ASICs—collection requires APIC REST API access, not direct leaf SNMP/CLI.
grandmaExplanation: We tally how often each tenant agreement actually welcomes traffic versus quietly refuses it—like a doorkeeper's scorecard—so quiet misconfigurations show up as shifting numbers before workloads stall and everyone blames the wrong software release.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco **APIC** clusters on supported **6.0(x)**, **6.1(x)**, or **6.2(x)** trains with HTTPS reachability from Splunk collectors on **443/tcp** and a dedicated **read-only** automation account whose **aaaUser** credentials isolate scripted polling from interactive admin sessions—overlapping **aaaRefresh.json** token rotations across shared identities collapse concurrent REST collectors.
• **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** ([Splunkbase **4022**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4022)) installed wherever **APIC** syslog terminates; the TA normalises mnemonic faults into **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`** but does **not** poll REST contract-statistics endpoints natively—supplement with **Splunk REST API Modular Input** (**rest_ta**, [Splunkbase **1546**](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1546)) or hardened Python modular inputs authenticated via `POST /api/aaaLogin.json` and refreshed through `/api/aaaRefresh.json` before **TTL** expiry.
• **Enable contract statistics** on each target tenant's contract by setting **statsEnabled** to **true** inside **vzBrCP** → **vzSubj** policy configurations or via REST `POST /api/mo/uni/tn-{tenant}/brc-{contract}.json` with `{"vzBrCP":{"attributes":{"statsEnabled":"true"}}}`. Without this flag **actrlRuleHit5min** counters remain zero even when dataplane traffic flows—this is the single most common missed prerequisite for this UC.
• **Enable ACL logging** per **vzSubj** at **log** or **logAndDeny** level so **acllogPermitL3Pkt** and **acllogDropL3Pkt** entries emit per-flow records including five-tuple fields (**sIp**, **dIp**, **sProt**, **dProt**, **prot**) plus **actrlRule** DN references; capacity-plan log volume because large-scale fabrics can generate **10,000–100,000** flow-log events per five-minute window per leaf pair under heavy east-west load.
• Roles used by Splunk searches must include **`srchIndexesAllowed`** covering `index=cisco_aci` (rename per governance) plus all scripted sourcetypes; **APIC** automation accounts require at minimum **read-only** admin role with access to **/api/class/actrlRuleHit5min.json**, **/api/class/actrlRule.json**, **/api/class/vzBrCP.json**, **/api/class/acllogPermitL3Pkt.json**, and **/api/class/acllogDropL3Pkt.json**.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) **Contract statistics polling** — Schedule HTTPS **GET** requests against **/api/class/actrlRuleHit5min.json?rsp-subtree-include=stats&rsp-subtree-class=actrlRuleHit5min** at **60–180** second cadence; each response returns per-**actrlRule** counters (**ingrPkts**, **egrPkts**, **ingrBytes**, **egrBytes**) with **DN** paths embedding **tn-{tenant}**, **brc-{contract}**, **subj-{subject}**, **flt-{filter}** segments for parent extraction—normalise outbound JSON into **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:contract_stats`** with extracted fields **actEntry** (permit/deny), **fltId**, **scopeId**, **sClass** (source pcTag), **dClass** (destination pcTag).
(2) **ACL flow-level logging** — When **ACL logging** is enabled on **vzSubj** objects, **APIC** emits **acllogPermitL3Pkt** and **acllogDropL3Pkt** entries per flow; ingest these via REST polling **/api/class/acllogPermitL3Pkt.json** and **/api/class/acllogDropL3Pkt.json** into **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:acl_log`** preserving five-tuple fields (**sIp**, **dIp**, **sProt**, **dProt**, **prot**) plus the **actrlRule** DN linking each flow back to its contract context—this sourcetype provides per-flow forensics that supplement the aggregate **actrlRuleHit5min** counters when operators need to identify exactly which application protocol a missing filter should cover.
(3) **Contract configuration snapshots** — Poll **/api/class/vzBrCP.json?rsp-subtree=full** including **vzSubj**, **vzFilter**, **vzEntry** children at **300–900** second intervals to maintain a configuration baseline in **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:contract_scope`**; key fields include **vzBrCP.name**, **vzSubj.name**, **vzSubj.consMatchT**/**provMatchT** (AtleastOne/All/None matching logic), **vzFilter.name**, **vzEntry.dFromPort**, **vzEntry.dToPort**, **vzEntry.prot**, **vzEntry.stateful**, and critically **vzSubj.statsEnabled** so Splunk can warn when statistics are accidentally disabled during configuration edits.
(4) **Syslog fault correlation** — Route **APIC** and leaf syslog through **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** for **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:syslog`**; filter for faults **F1394** (contract-deny hit counter exceeds threshold—this is APIC's native alarm for heavy deny activity), **F0467** (deployed-config-mismatch indicating contract scope divergence between intended and rendered policy), **F2301** (EPG contract binding failure—consumer or provider attachment rejected), **F0952** (filter TCAM programming failure on leaf—compiled rules did not install in hardware), plus syslog mnemonic **ACLLOG-5-ACLLOG_PKTLOG** confirming flow-level logging pipeline health.
(5) **Fault instance polling** — Poll **/api/class/faultInst.json** filtered by `query-target-filter=or(eq(faultInst.code,"F1394"),eq(faultInst.code,"F0467"),eq(faultInst.code,"F2301"),eq(faultInst.code,"F0952"))` into **`sourcetype=cisco:aci:fault`** capturing structured fault records with **severity**, **lc** (lifecycle: soaking/raised/raised-clearing/retaining), **created**, **lastTransition** timestamps plus the full DN path identifying the affected contract, tenant, and leaf.
(6) **Volume guidance** — Per-contract-subject **actrlRuleHit5min** counters generate approximately **2–4 KB** per poll per compiled rule; a fabric with **500** compiled rules at **120s** intervals produces roughly **1,500–3,000** events per five-minute window. **ACL flow logs** scale with traffic diversity rather than volume—budget **5–20×** the contract-stats volume when **log** mode is enabled on busy subjects across multiple leaves. Shard REST collectors across **APIC** cluster members when response latency exceeds **8** seconds consistently.

Step 2 — Splunk SPL search and alert configuration
Bookmark **`aci_uc1819_contract_ratio`** referencing the canonical SPL bundled in this UC—schedule cron `*/5 * * * *` over `earliest=-24h@m`, throttle **tenant_tag** + **contract_tag** pairs for **15** minutes unless **tot_deny** doubles between consecutive windows.

```spl
index=cisco_aci (
  sourcetype IN ("cisco:aci:contract_stats","cisco:aci:acl_log","cisco:aci:contract_scope")
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:syslog" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952|ACLLOG|actrl|implicitRule)"))
  OR (sourcetype="cisco:aci:fault" AND match(_raw,"(?i)(F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952)"))
 ) earliest=-24h@m
| eval lane=case(sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_stats","stats",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:acl_log","flow",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:contract_scope","scope",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:syslog","sf",sourcetype=="cisco:aci:fault","fi",1=1,"misc")
| eval dn_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(dn,distinguished_name,rule_dn,"")))
| rex field=dn_lc "tn-(?<tenant_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "brc-(?<contract_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "subj-(?<subject_tag>[^/]+)"
| rex field=dn_lc "flt-(?<filter_tag>[^/]+)"
| eval act_raw=lower(trim(coalesce(actEntry,action,rule_action,"")))
| eval rule_class=case(
    match(act_raw,"^permit"), "explicit_permit",
    match(dn_lc,"implicitrule") AND match(act_raw,"^deny"), "implicit_deny",
    match(act_raw,"^deny"), "explicit_deny",
    lane=="flow" AND match(act_raw,"^permit"), "flow_permit",
    lane=="flow" AND match(act_raw,"^deny"), "flow_deny",
    lane IN ("sf","fi"), "fault_signal",
    1=1, "unclassified")
| eval pkts_permit=case(
    rule_class IN ("explicit_permit","flow_permit") AND isnotnull(tonumber(coalesce(ingrPkts,ingress_packets,pkts_in,permit_count))), tonumber(coalesce(ingrPkts,ingress_packets,pkts_in,permit_count)),
    rule_class IN ("explicit_permit","flow_permit") AND lane=="flow", 1,
    1=1, 0)
| eval pkts_deny=case(
    rule_class IN ("implicit_deny","explicit_deny","flow_deny") AND isnotnull(tonumber(coalesce(denyPkts,deny_count,drop_count,ingrPkts))), tonumber(coalesce(denyPkts,deny_count,drop_count,ingrPkts)),
    rule_class IN ("implicit_deny","explicit_deny","flow_deny") AND lane=="flow", 1,
    1=1, 0)
| eval bytes_total=tonumber(coalesce(ingrBytes,ingress_bytes,bytes_in,0))+tonumber(coalesce(egrBytes,egress_bytes,bytes_out,0))
| eval flt_id=trim(coalesce(fltId,filter_id,""))
| eval scope_id=trim(coalesce(scopeId,scope_id,""))
| eval src_pctag=trim(coalesce(sClass,src_class_id,sPcTag,""))
| eval dst_pctag=trim(coalesce(dClass,dst_class_id,dPcTag,""))
| eval fault_code=if(lane IN ("sf","fi"),trim(coalesce(fault_code,code,"")),"")
| bin _time span=15m as win15
| stats sum(pkts_permit) as tot_permit sum(pkts_deny) as tot_deny max(bytes_total) as peak_bytes values(rule_class) as rule_classes values(filter_tag) as filter_tags values(fault_code) as fault_codes dc(flt_id) as unique_filters latest(src_pctag) as s_pctag latest(dst_pctag) as d_pctag count as evt_count by win15 tenant_tag contract_tag subject_tag
| eval ratio=round(if((tot_permit+tot_deny)==0,null(),tot_permit/(tot_permit+tot_deny)),4)
| eval implicit_dom=if(mvfind(rule_classes,"implicit_deny")>=0 AND tot_deny>tot_permit,"YES","no")
| eval contract_path=tenant_tag."/".contract_tag."/".subject_tag
| eval risk_deny_flood=if(tot_deny>500 AND ratio<0.5,1,0)
| eval risk_implicit_storm=if(implicit_dom=="YES" AND tot_deny>1000,1,0)
| eval risk_ratio_collapse=if(ratio<0.3 AND (tot_permit+tot_deny)>100,1,0)
| eval risk_zero_permit=if(tot_permit==0 AND tot_deny>0,1,0)
| eval risk_fault=if(mvcount(fault_codes)>0 AND match(mvjoin(fault_codes,","),"F1394|F0467|F2301|F0952"),1,0)
| where risk_deny_flood=1 OR risk_implicit_storm=1 OR risk_ratio_collapse=1 OR risk_zero_permit=1 OR risk_fault=1
| sort -tot_deny -evt_count
| head 250
| table win15 tenant_tag contract_path ratio tot_permit tot_deny implicit_dom filter_tags s_pctag d_pctag fault_codes risk_deny_flood risk_implicit_storm risk_ratio_collapse peak_bytes evt_count
```

**Understanding this SPL — pipeline walkthrough and threshold rationale**

**Lane classification** — The five sourcetypes each serve a distinct evidence role: **stats** provides aggregate hardware counters, **flow** provides per-flow five-tuple forensics, **scope** provides configuration context, **sf** provides syslog fault narratives, **fi** provides structured fault lifecycle records. Merging them into one pipeline lets a single alert correlate ratio shifts with configuration changes and fault activity.

**Rule classification** — The **rule_class** eval distinguishes **explicit_permit** (traffic matched a **vzFilter**/**vzEntry** permit rule), **implicit_deny** (traffic hit the auto-generated catch-all deny where no contract exists between the EPG pair), **explicit_deny** (traffic matched a **vzTaboo** or deny-action filter), **flow_permit**/**flow_deny** (per-flow ACL log entries), and **fault_signal** (syslog/faultInst records). The **implicit_deny** class is the primary signal for policy coverage gaps.

**Ratio calculation** — `ratio = tot_permit / (tot_permit + tot_deny)` expresses contract effectiveness as a proportion. A ratio of **1.0** means every packet matched an explicit permit; a ratio approaching **0.0** means nearly all traffic is being denied. **Threshold rationale**: `ratio < 0.5` flags contracts where more traffic is denied than permitted—unusual for production contracts where the policy intent is to enable communication. `ratio < 0.3` triggers **risk_ratio_collapse** for critical investigation. `tot_deny > 500` filters out low-volume noise from test tenants. Tune thresholds per tenant criticality—shared-services tenants may tolerate ratio **0.7** while production-critical tenants should alert at **0.9**.

**Implicit deny dominance** — When **implicit_dom** is **YES**, the denial source is the catch-all rule rather than an explicit deny filter, meaning traffic is flowing between EPG pairs with **no** contract at all. This is categorically different from a **vzTaboo** deny—the former indicates a missing policy relationship, the latter indicates intentional blocking.

**Macros recommended** — Wrap **threshold_deny_volume**, **threshold_ratio_warn**, **threshold_ratio_crit** inside `macros.conf` so tuning stays declarative per tenant without editing the search body.

Step 3 — Validate against APIC and leaf hardware
(1) **APIC GUI contract statistics** — Navigate **Tenants → {tenant} → Application Profiles → {AP} → Application EPGs → {EPG} → Contracts** and expand the **Statistics** tab for each contract subject. The **5min** counters for permit and deny should match Splunk's **tot_permit** and **tot_deny** within one poll-interval lag (typically **60–180** seconds). If APIC shows counters but Splunk shows zero, verify **statsEnabled=true** on the **vzSubj** object.
(2) **Leaf CLI verification** — SSH to a border leaf and run `show zoning-rule scope <scopeId>` to display compiled **actrlRule** entries with their **sClass**, **dClass**, **fltId**, and **action** (permit/deny/redirect/copy). Cross-check the **action** column against Splunk's **rule_class** classification. Run `show system internal policy-mgr stats` to confirm hardware counters increment—zeroed counters despite active traffic indicate the hardware programming path (**F0952**) failed silently.
(3) **Implicit deny identification** — In APIC, navigate **Fabric → Access Policies → Policies → Troubleshooting → Contract Checker** and enter the source and destination EPG pair. The tool identifies whether traffic between them would hit an explicit contract or the implicit deny. Compare this against Splunk's **implicit_dom** flag for the same EPG pair (identified by **s_pctag**/**d_pctag**).
(4) **ACL log correlation** — When **risk_ratio_collapse** fires, drill into `sourcetype=cisco:aci:acl_log rule_class=flow_deny` for the same **contract_tag** to extract the denied five-tuples (**sIp**, **dIp**, **prot**, **dport_hint**). Compare these against the **vzEntry** rules in the contract's filter chain—if the denied **dport_hint** falls outside all **vzEntry.dFromPort**/**vzEntry.dToPort** ranges, the filter needs a new entry.
(5) **Configuration baseline diff** — Compare the latest **cisco:aci:contract_scope** snapshot against a known-good baseline (archived nightly via `| outputlookup aci_contract_baseline.csv`) to detect filter deletions, port-range narrowing, or **consMatchT** changes that explain ratio shifts without requiring manual APIC GUI navigation.

Step 4 — Operationalise dashboards and alert routing
(1) **Dashboard layout** — Present **ratio** timechart as the top row with an overlay of **tot_deny** magnitude; second row shows the **tenant_tag × contract_tag** heatmap so operators identify which contracts drift first; third row shows **implicit_dom=YES** contracts as a dedicated table since these indicate missing policy relationships rather than misconfigured filters; fourth row provides drilldown into **acllog** five-tuples for the selected contract.
(2) **Alert routing** — Send **risk_ratio_collapse** (ratio < **0.3**) to the **ACI fabric team** Slack/Teams channel with contract path and affected EPG pcTags; send **risk_implicit_storm** to the **application architecture** team since missing contracts typically require application-tier awareness to resolve; send **risk_fault** (**F0952** TCAM failures) to the **fabric infrastructure** team because TCAM exhaustion affects all tenants on the affected leaf.
(3) **Suppression** — Maintain a **contract_maint_windows.csv** lookup keyed by **tenant_tag** + **contract_tag** with **window_start**, **window_end** fields populated from change-management feeds. The search can join this lookup and suppress alerts during documented migration windows.
(4) **Runbook integration** — Document remediation ownership by **tenant_tag**. Contracts in the **common** tenant affect all VRFs and require **fabric administrator** approval to modify. Contracts in application-specific tenants route to the application team's on-call. Include deep links to APIC **Contract Checker** and **Faults** pages.
(5) **Capacity review cadence** — Monthly review of ratio trends per contract to identify gradually degrading contracts where ratio declines slowly over weeks as new microservices deploy without corresponding filter updates—schedule a **saved search** exporting thirty-day ratio averages per contract to a summary index.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting and alert design

• **Zero actrlRuleHit5min events despite active traffic** — The most common cause is **statsEnabled=false** on the **vzSubj** object. Verify via REST `GET /api/mo/uni/tn-{tenant}/brc-{contract}/subj-{subject}.json?query-target=self` and confirm `"statsEnabled":"yes"`. If statistics were recently enabled, counters begin populating after the next **actrlRuleHit5min** evaluation cycle (typically within five minutes). If still zero, check the **APIC** **Statistics** policy at **Fabric → Fabric Policies → Policies → Monitoring → Statistics** to confirm the default collection policy is enabled.

• **All ratios show 1.0 (no deny events)** — Either **ACL logging** is disabled (only permits are visible through **actrlRuleHit5min** but denies are not counted separately), or the fabric uses **vzAny** with **prefGroupMemb=include** so all EPG-pair communication is implicitly permitted. Verify with `show zoning-rule` on a leaf—if only permit entries exist and no implicit deny rule appears in the scope, the VRF is operating in **preferred-group** mode where deny counters are structurally absent.

• **Ratio collapsed but no application impact reported** — The denied traffic may be scanner noise, monitoring probes, or broadcast/multicast that applications do not depend on. Drill into **cisco:aci:acl_log** denied flows and classify the **prot**/**dport_hint** values. Common benign deny targets include **ICMP** echo from monitoring systems, **NetBIOS** broadcasts (**137–139/UDP**), **mDNS** (**5353/UDP**), and **SSDP** (**1900/UDP**).

• **Ratio oscillates on a predictable schedule** — Correlate with batch-job schedules. Many ACI deployments have contracts scoped to specific application tiers; when batch jobs run outside those tiers, they hit implicit denies. If the oscillation aligns with a known schedule (nightly ETL, weekly backup windows), either extend the contract filter to cover the batch traffic or suppress the alert window.

• **F0952 TCAM programming failures** — The leaf has exhausted its TCAM policy region. Run `vsh_lc -c "show platform internal hal l2 phase4 policy-stats"` on the affected leaf to check utilisation. TCAM capacity varies by hardware generation: **Nexus 9300-FX** supports approximately **64K** policy entries versus **128K** on **Nexus 9300-GX2**. If TCAM is full, consolidate overlapping filters—this directly affects contract ratio analysis because unprogrammed rules mean traffic that *should* be permitted falls through to the implicit deny instead.

• **Stale collector data after aaaRefresh failure** — When the REST collector's **aaaRefresh.json** call returns HTTP **401** or **403** (token expired or account locked), subsequent **actrlRuleHit5min** polls fail silently if error handling is not instrumented—Splunk continues serving stale counters from the last successful poll, masking real ratio changes. Check `splunkd.log` on the Heavy Forwarder for `aaaRefresh` errors and configure collector-level alerting on consecutive failed polls.

• **Duplicate counters from multiple collectors** — If two REST collectors poll the same **APIC** cluster member for **actrlRuleHit5min**, Splunk double-counts both permits and denies; the ratio itself stays correct (since both numerator and denominator double) but **tot_deny** volume-based thresholds trigger prematurely. Stamp each collector with a **collector_id** metadata field and deduplicate before the **stats** stage if this situation is suspected.

• **pcTag changes after EPG migration** — When an EPG is migrated to a different bridge domain or application profile, the APIC may reallocate its **pcTag** (sClass/dClass). Historical **actrlRuleHit5min** data keyed by the old pcTag becomes orphaned—ratio calculations show the old contract as suddenly having zero permits while a new contract (with the new pcTag) appears. Maintain a **pcTag_history.csv** lookup mapping EPG DNs to historical pcTags for continuity.

• **Contract-scope mismatch across pods in multi-pod** — In multi-pod ACI fabrics, contract statistics are pod-local—if collectors poll only Pod-1's APIC but the traffic flows through Pod-2's leaves, Splunk sees zero counters for those contracts. Shard collectors across all pods' APIC endpoints or use the **inter-pod** API proxy on the **IPN**-facing APIC.

• **Alert design guidance** — Schedule at `*/5 * * * *` (every five minutes) over `earliest=-1h@m` for alerting, `earliest=-24h@m` for trending. Use **triggered alert** type with suppression on **contract_path** for **15** minutes. For critical production tenants, chain with a **Splunk SOAR** playbook that automatically queries APIC for the current contract configuration and attaches the diff to the incident ticket. For capacity planning, schedule a weekly summary `| timechart span=1d avg(ratio) by contract_path` exporting to a summary index for thirty-day trend analysis.
